rationale: The reproduced prompt (muse:L24-27, L56-58, L64) explicitly demands arXiv/PubMed/OpenAlex sources, >=5 references with direct citations, a citation-consistency check, and six named Phases (muse:L29-47). The 'no instructions' claim is contradicted by the experiment's own prompt.
required_evidence: The prompt actually sent to the model.
source_quote: The Muse Spark 1.1  model receives a complex prompt and produces a structured response. No epistemic instructions (requests for verification, sources, stages, etc.) are provided.
text: Muse Spark received no epistemic instructions (no requests for verification, sources, or stages).
uncertainty: None on the direction of the conflict; the prompt is printed in full.

rationale: 'External' holds literally — it is a separate call, here even a different model family (Muse Spark vs. a Llama validator). But 'independent' is not documented on any axis: the validator receives exactly the generation documents (code:evaluator_prompt L24-28), is non-adversarial and not reproducibly specified. A single external llm.invoke does not by itself REFUTE independence — but it does not ESTABLISH it either; here the 'independent' label is a methodological misclassification, not a demonstrated property (the README concedes the shared bias, doc:readme L133).
required_evidence: The validator's implementation and the inputs it receives.
source_quote: External Validation The generated text is sent to MarCognity-AI. An epistemic analysis protocol is applied based on: ...
text: The validation is external and independent (any 'independent external validator').
uncertainty: 'External' is uncontested; only the *documented* independence (organizational / model-side / evidence-side) is missing.

rationale: No model version, temperature, seed, or retrieval snapshot is given for either Muse Spark or the validator; LLM calls are non-deterministic by default. Reproducibility is asserted, not demonstrated. Per the case-study rules we do not speculate about Muse Spark's unknown settings.
required_evidence: Model version, decoding settings, seed, retrieval snapshot, run logs.
source_quote: The procedure is fully reproducible and can be replicated with any closed autoregressive model and any independent external validator.
text: The procedure is fully reproducible.
uncertainty: High — the run parameters are simply absent.

rationale: PubMed indexes biomedical literature; the claims are legal philosophy (Kelsen/Hart/Bobbio). 'Supported by the PubMed document' is a domain-mismatched source, and no document title or passage is named. A database hit is not evidence.
required_evidence: A primary legal-philosophy source with a quoted passage per claim.
source_quote: CLAIM: "Legal certainty and legal effectiveness are two fundamental concepts..." STATUS: VERIFIED REASON: The claim is supported by the provided documents, specifically the PubMed document ... [five claims, all STATUS: VERIFIED, each 'supported by the PubMed document'].
text: Five general legal claims are 'VERIFIED' because supported by 'the PubMed document'.
uncertainty: We cannot inspect the retrieved abstract (not printed); the mismatch is established by the validator's own wording and the code path.

rationale: The text carries eight well-formed references (muse:L154-161: Kelsen 1960, Hart 1961, Bobbio 1993, Fuller 1969, Luhmann 1986, Guastini 1986, Tamanaha 2001, Kaplow 1992). 'No citations found' is a false negative of the regex extractor (code:extract_citations). The deeper problem is the unresolved pipeline inconsistency C2: the Skeptical Agent asserts source support without naming a source or passage, while the citation checker finds no verifiable references, and both are merged without a consistency check (code:agent_metacognition L48-66) — 'VERIFIED' and 'no citations' need not logically contradict, but here they stand un-reconciled.
required_evidence: The reference list in the text and the citation extractor's behaviour.
source_quote:
VERIFIED BIBLIOGRAPHIC REFERENCES
No citations found or verifiable in the text.
text: No citations were found or verifiable in the text.
uncertainty: Whether each reference is bibliographically correct is NOT asserted here (not independently checked); only that references plainly exist.

rationale: This self-observation is correct and matches the code: there is no domain gating on the source set (code:agent_metacognition L48 feeds whatever was retrieved). The experiment correctly *notices* the mismatch — then, in EB-02, mis-reads what it implies.
required_evidence: The validator's retrieval configuration and report.
source_quote: the validator anchored its verification to databases that were clearly inconsistent with the domain being addressed (assigning the authorship of concepts from the philosophy of law to PubMed sources).
text: The validator anchored verification to databases inconsistent with the domain (legal philosophy attributed to PubMed).

rationale: A theoretical reading of the one observed failure. Plausible as a framing but presented as a demonstrated law; a single mismatched-retrieval instance cannot establish a structural tendency.
required_evidence: A controlled study over many items with a falsifiable metric.
source_quote: The test proved so profound that it revealed the Epistemic Boundary even within the validator itself, proving that statistical consistency tends to structurally override historical truth. This phenomenon empirically demonstrates that the residual uncertainty regime is not an isolated flaw of the generative model (Muse Spark), but an intrinsic characteristic of the autoregressive architecture, which also influences the automated evaluation and monitoring processes.
text: Statistical consistency tends to structurally override historical truth.
uncertainty: No sample size, controls, or falsifier — n=1 anecdote.

rationale: 'Empirically demonstrates ... intrinsic' is unsupported by one uncontrolled run. It also over-reaches beyond MarCognity's OWN Epistemic Boundary doc (doc:epistemic_boundary L119-122: 'latent construct, not directly observable ... causal mechanisms not identified ... further controlled experimental validation is required'). The forum conclusion is stronger than the repository it cites.
required_evidence: Multi-model, multi-domain controlled evaluation with stated falsification conditions.
source_quote: The test proved so profound that it revealed the Epistemic Boundary even within the validator itself, proving that statistical consistency tends to structurally override historical truth. This phenomenon empirically demonstrates that the residual uncertainty regime is not an isolated flaw of the generative model (Muse Spark), but an intrinsic characteristic of the autoregressive architecture, which also influences the automated evaluation and monitoring processes.
text: This empirically demonstrates the residual uncertainty regime is an intrinsic characteristic of the autoregressive architecture.
uncertainty: This is the self-sealing move (see contradictions.md / analysis).

rationale: A coherent, broadly standard doctrinal definition. Marked as an interpretive framing, not adjudicated true/false; its verifiability rides on the attribution ATTR-01, which the material does not evidence.
required_evidence: Standard doctrinal sources; the specific attribution is a separate claim (ATTR-01).
source_quote: Legal certainty (Rechtssicherheit, rule of law in its formal sense) indicates the possibility for the legal subject to predict with reasonable reliability the legal consequences of their actions. It can be analytically broken down into three dimensions, following the systematization of Guastini and Bobbio
text: Legal certainty = predictability of legal consequences, broken into knowability / stability / determinacy.

rationale: As DEF-01: a reasoned definitional framing; the Kelsen/Di Lucia attribution is the checkable part and is unevidenced in the material.
required_evidence: Standard doctrinal sources; attribution handled in ATTR-02.
source_quote: Effectiveness of law (Wirksamkeit, efficacy) indicates instead the degree of empirical realization of the law. It is also three-dimensional, according to the classical distinction of Kelsen taken up by Di Lucia
text: Effectiveness of law = degree of empirical realization; three-dimensional (compliance / application / enforcement).

rationale: A checkable attribution routed to legal philosophy. The experiment provides no passage from Guastini or Bobbio; the PubMed 'verification' is domain-mismatched. DESi does not itself adjudicate the attribution here.
required_evidence: The cited authors' texts, with locating passages.
source_quote: Legal certainty (Rechtssicherheit, rule of law in its formal sense) indicates the possibility for the legal subject to predict with reasonable reliability the legal consequences of their actions. It can be analytically broken down into three dimensions, following the systematization of Guastini and Bobbio
text: The three-dimensional systematization of certainty follows Guastini and Bobbio.
uncertainty: Guastini (1986) is in the text's own bibliography; that is a citation, not a verified locating passage.

rationale: No passage supplied; Di Lucia is not even in the bibliography. Routed to legal philosophy; unevidenced in the material.
required_evidence: Kelsen's and Di Lucia's texts, with locating passages.
source_quote: Effectiveness of law (Wirksamkeit, efficacy) indicates instead the degree of empirical realization of the law. It is also three-dimensional, according to the classical distinction of Kelsen taken up by Di Lucia
text: The three-dimensional distinction of effectiveness is Kelsen's, taken up by Di Lucia.

rationale: A quotation mark raises the evidence bar to an exact page/edition. The material provides none; the validator checked nothing quote-specific. Not adjudicated here.
required_evidence: The exact locus in Kelsen (Reine Rechtslehre) — a direct quote demands the highest provenance.
source_quote: For Kelsen, effectiveness is a condition of validity of the legal order as a whole ... "a legal order is considered valid if its norms are on the whole effective".
text: Kelsen: "a legal order is considered valid if its norms are on the whole effective".
uncertainty: Direct quotes are the most provenance-hungry claim type and the least served by abstract-level retrieval.

rationale: As QUOTE-01: exact-locus evidence required and absent.
required_evidence: The exact locus in Bobbio (Teoria generale del diritto).
source_quote: Bobbio ... "the problem of the effectiveness of law is the problem of the correspondence between the prescriptive model and actual behavior".
text: Bobbio: "the problem of the effectiveness of law is the problem of the correspondence between the prescriptive model and actual behavior".

rationale: A crisply checkable doctrinal fact (Fuller's eight principles of legality), routed to a primary legal-philosophy source. The experiment evidences it with nothing. Note (analyst, not evidence): this is well-known and the enumeration looks correct, but this case study did not fetch Fuller to confirm it.
required_evidence: Fuller, The Morality of Law (1969), ch. 2.
source_quote: Fuller lists eight canons of certainty (generality, promulgation, non-retroactivity, clarity, non-contradiction, possibility, constancy, congruence between norm and action)
text: Fuller lists eight canons of certainty (enumerated).
uncertainty: Marked unverifiable to respect the rule that recognition is not verification without a cited passage.

rationale: The text itself flags it as heuristic (muse:L88 'in a heuristic way'). With no derivation or empirical calibration it is an author's own construction — neither true nor false, and specifically NOT to be reported as 'verified'.
required_evidence: Not applicable — a heuristic construction, not an empirical finding.
source_quote: C = alpha*Ch + beta*St + gamma*Ge  where Ch is semantic clarity, St temporal stability, Ge generality, with alpha+beta+gamma = 1.
text: Certainty index C = alpha*Ch + beta*St + gamma*Ge (alpha+beta+gamma=1).

rationale: As HEUR-01: an unvalidated own-construction weighting; a category the binary VERIFIED/FAILURE scheme cannot represent.
required_evidence: Not applicable — heuristic construction.
source_quote: E = w1*Comp + w2*Appl + w3*Esec  where Comp is compliance, Appl jurisdictional application, Esec forced enforcement.
text: Effectiveness index E = w1*Comp + w2*Appl + w3*Esec.

rationale: A proposed functional form with no specified functional shape or data. Heuristic proposal.
required_evidence: Not applicable — heuristic construction.
source_quote: There is an interdependence function E = f(C, K, L) where K is institutional capital and L social legitimation.
text: Interdependence function E = f(C, K, L), with K institutional capital, L social legitimation.

rationale: A theoretical synthesis attributed to Coase/Posner-style analysis. Presented as reasoning, not measured; reasonable but unmeasured in the material.
required_evidence: Empirical law-and-economics literature testing the threshold.
source_quote: certainty and effectiveness are in a curvilinear, not linear, relationship. Up to a threshold, increasing determinacy increases compliance ... Beyond the threshold, hyper-regulation produces rigidity
text: Certainty and effectiveness stand in a curvilinear (threshold) relationship (economic analysis of law).

rationale: A contestable empirical-historical claim with no source in the material. Plausible as illustration; not evidenced, and 'effectiveness/certainty' are used here without the operational metrics the text itself proposes.
required_evidence: Empirical/legal studies of the DPCM litigation and compliance record.
source_quote: during the COVID-19 pandemic, the DPCMs in Italy showed high immediate effectiveness through rapidly modifiable decrees, but low certainty
text: During COVID-19, Italian DPCMs showed high effectiveness but low certainty.
uncertainty: Empirically arguable both ways; no data given.

rationale: A juristic-factual claim plus an interpretive framing ('component of certainty'). The existence and scope of a 'right to explanation' is itself legally contested; the material cites nothing. Routed to positive law, unevidenced.
required_evidence: The AI Act text and secondary legal analysis of any 'right to explanation'.
source_quote: The EU AI Act proposal introduces the right to explainability as a new component of certainty.
text: The EU AI Act introduces a right to explainability as a new component of certainty.

rationale: The event (The DAO, 2016) is well established in general knowledge and the date matches; but the material supplies no source, and a database/common knowledge is not evidence under this protocol. Not adjudicated here.
required_evidence: A primary/secondary account of the 2016 DAO incident.
source_quote: as the case of The DAO hack 2016 demonstrates, absolute rigidification produces new uncertainty when code diverges from social expectations.
text: The DAO hack of 2016 illustrates how absolute code-rigidity produces new uncertainty.

rationale: A value judgement about what legal science 'ought' to do. Not verifiable against sources; correctly typed as normative rather than forced into VERIFIED/FAILURE.
required_evidence: Not applicable — a normative recommendation, not a truth-apt claim.
source_quote: The task of a mature legal science is not to resolve the tension, but to design institutions capable of managing it in a reflective and justifiable way.
text: The task of a mature legal science is not to resolve the certainty/effectiveness tension but to design institutions that manage it.
